segments.text: Lesson 8.

translations.zh-Hans: 第8课。

segments.text: Standards.

translations.zh-Hans: 贸易标准。

segments.text: First listen and then answer the following question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: What makes trading between rich countries difficult?

translations.zh-Hans: 什么使富国之间的贸易变得困难？

segments.text: Chickens slaughtered in the United States, claim officials in Brussels, are not fit to grace European tables.

translations.zh-Hans: 布鲁塞尔的官员声称，在美国宰杀的鸡不配登上欧洲人的餐桌。

segments.text: No, say the Americans.

translations.zh-Hans: 美国人说，不对。

segments.text: Our fowl are fine.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们的鸡很好。

segments.text: We simply clean them in a different way.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们只是采用了不同的清洗方式。

segments.text: These days, it is differences in national regulations, far more than tariffs, that put sand in the wheels of trade between rich countries.

translations.zh-Hans: 如今，阻碍富国之间贸易的，与其说是关税，不如说是各国法规的差异。

segments.text: It is not just farmers who are complaining.

translations.zh-Hans: 抱怨的并不只有农民。

segments.text: An electric razor that meets the European Union's safety standards must be approved by American testers before it can be sold in the United States.

translations.zh-Hans: 一款符合欧盟安全标准的电动剃须刀，在美国销售前仍必须获得美国检测机构批准。

segments.text: And an American-made dialysis machine needs the EU's okay before it hits the market in Europe.

translations.zh-Hans: 而美国制造的透析机进入欧洲市场前，也需要欧盟认可。

segments.text: As it happens, a razor that is safe in Europe is unlikely to electrocute Americans.

translations.zh-Hans: 事实上，在欧洲使用安全的剃须刀不大可能把美国人电死。

segments.text: So, ask businesses on both sides of the Atlantic, why have two lots of tests where one would do?

translations.zh-Hans: 因此，大西洋两岸的企业都问：一套测试足够，为什么还要做两套？

segments.text: Politicians agree, in principle, so America and the EU have been trying to reach a deal which would eliminate the need to double-test many products.

translations.zh-Hans: 政界人士原则上表示赞同，因此美国和欧盟一直试图达成协议，取消许多产品接受双重检测的必要。

segments.text: They hope to finish in time for a trade summit between America and the EU on May 28.

translations.zh-Hans: 他们希望赶在5月28日欧美贸易峰会之前完成。

segments.text: Although negotiators are optimistic, the details are complex enough that they may be hard-pressed to get a deal at all.

translations.zh-Hans: 尽管谈判代表持乐观态度，但细节相当复杂，他们可能很难达成任何协议。

segments.text: Why?

translations.zh-Hans: 为什么？

segments.text: One difficulty is to construct the agreements.

translations.zh-Hans: 一个困难在于协议的构建方式。

segments.text: The Americans would happily reach one accord on standards for medical devices and then hammer out different packs covering, say, electronic goods and drug manufacturing.

translations.zh-Hans: 美国人乐于先就医疗设备标准达成一项协议，再分别敲定涵盖电子产品、药品制造等领域的其他协议。

segments.text: The EU, following fine continental traditions, wants agreement on general principles which could be applied to many types of products and perhaps extended to other countries.

translations.zh-Hans: 欧盟则遵循欧洲大陆的优良传统，希望就能适用于多种产品、或许还能推广到其他国家的一般原则达成协议。
